codeReview.summary: Clean, minimal fix. The single-line change from `exclude` to `extend-exclude` in `[tool.ruff]` correctly preserves Ruff's default exclusion list (which includes `.git`) while keeping the repo-specific benchmark directory exclusions. The regression test is well-structured: it asserts both that `exclude` is absent and that `extend-exclude` contains the expected list. The helper refactor (`_pyproject_payload()` extraction) is sensible and improves reusability in the test module. Worker followed TDD: verified the test failed before the fix and passed after. All three local gates (ruff, mypy, pytest) passed in both the main worktree and a clean validation worktree. No code quality issues, no bugs, no security concerns.

issues.description: The `lint` command in services.yaml still uses `uv run ruff check . --exclude .git`, which was the old workaround. After this fix, the `--exclude .git` flag is redundant since `extend-exclude` preserves Ruff's default `.git` exclusion. The command should be updated to `uv run ruff check .` to reflect the fix and avoid confusion.

sharedStateObservations.observation: services.yaml `lint` command still includes the `--exclude .git` workaround (`uv run ruff check . --exclude .git`) that this fix was specifically designed to eliminate. The command should be simplified to `uv run ruff check .` now that `extend-exclude` preserves Ruff's default `.git` exclusion.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml line 4: `lint: uv run ruff check . --exclude .git`. Compare to the feature's stated goal: 'change exclude to extend-exclude so stale .git worktree dirs do not cause F811 lint failures' and verification step: 'Run uv run ruff check . (without --exclude .git) and confirm it passes'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used `git cherry-pick` instead of the documented `git merge` path in the backend-worker skill's 'Final Step: Merge to Main' section. The deviation was justified (primary main checkout had unrelated dirty state from other mission work). Worker's skillFeedback suggests documenting cherry-pick as an approved alternative.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.deviations[1]: 'Validated in a clean feature worktree, then restored the touched files in the dirty main checkout and cherry-picked the validated commit onto main instead of doing git checkout main && git merge <branch>'. Skill SKILL.md only documents merge-based flows.

summary: Pass. The fix correctly changes `exclude` to `extend-exclude` in pyproject.toml's [tool.ruff] section, preserving Ruff's default `.git` exclusion while keeping repo-specific benchmark directory exclusions. A well-written regression test was added. All local gates passed. One non-blocking observation: services.yaml still carries the now-redundant `--exclude .git` workaround in its lint command.
